findings.summary: Avalo Therapeutics announced the completion of enrollment in its Phase 2 LOTUS trial evaluating AVTX-009 (abdakibart), an anti-IL-1 beta monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of moderate-to-severe Hidradenitis Suppurativa. Topline data is expected in 2026.

AVTX-002 (quisovalimab), a monoclonal antibody targeting LIGHT (TNFSF14), is being explored for its potential in treating non-eosinophilic asthma and COPD, with presentations on its role in airway inflammation delivered at the ATS 2023 Respiratory Innovation Summit.

Avalo Therapeutics' investor materials have referenced BAFF inhibition (e.g., Benlysta) as a relevant therapeutic benchmark or mechanism of interest in the landscape of chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseases.

MoonLake Immunotherapeutics reported positive Week 40 results from the Phase 3 VELA-1 and VELA-2 trials of sonelokimab in Hidradenitis Suppurativa, demonstrating significant efficacy in achieving HiSCR75 and HiSCR90 response rates compared to placebo.

Sonelokimab is a trivalent Nanobody that selectively binds to both IL-17A and IL-17F. It is the lead asset for MoonLake, showing promise in Hidradenitis Suppurativa and Psoriatic Arthritis (Phase 2 ARGO trial).

Oruka Therapeutics announced positive interim Phase 1 data for ORKA-002, an IL-17A/F inhibitor, which demonstrated a favorable safety profile and potent cytokine neutralization. The company plans to initiate a Phase 2 trial in Hidradenitis Suppurativa in 2026.

Scientific characterization of ORKA-002 presented at meetings like EULAR and ACR highlights its high affinity for IL-17A and IL-17F and its potential for infrequent maintenance dosing (e.g., once every 6 months) in diseases like HS.

Insmed announced the discontinuation of its brensocatib program in Hidradenitis Suppurativa following the Phase 2b CEDAR study, which failed to meet primary or secondary efficacy endpoints at both the 10 mg and 40 mg dose levels.

Data from Insmed's ASPEN study on brensocatib (a DPP1 inhibitor) in non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis showed consistent reduction in exacerbations across subgroups, including patients with comorbid COPD.

Insmed is evaluating Treprostinil Palmitil (TPIP) in Phase 2 trials for pulmonary hypertension associated with interstitial lung disease (PH-ILD). PH-ILD is a common and severe complication of Systemic Sclerosis (SSc).
Aclaris Therapeutics announced that its Phase 2a study of zunsemetinib (ATI-450) for moderate to severe hidradenitis suppurativa did not meet its primary or secondary efficacy endpoints.

Aclaris Therapeutics entered into an exclusive global license agreement with CTTQ for the development and commercialization of bosakitug (ATI-045), an ITK/TXK/JAK3 inhibitor, for COPD and other indications.

AnaptysBio reported topline data from its HARP Phase 2 clinical trial of imsidolimab (IL-36R inhibitor) in moderate-to-severe HS. The study did not meet its primary endpoint, leading to the discontinuation of imsidolimab's development for HS.
Itepekimab, an IL-33 antagonist developed by AnaptysBio in partnership with Sanofi/Regeneron, has been evaluated in Phase 2 trials for COPD, showing efficacy in former smokers.
AnaptysBio's pipeline includes ANB030, an anti-IL-7R antibody currently being developed for inflammatory diseases like alopecia areata and potentially others.
Recent biopharma updates for AnaptysBio mentioned the competitive landscape of B-cell therapies like BAFF/APRIL inhibitors while discussing the company's development of ANB032 (BTLA agonist).
UCB announced Phase 3 data for bimekizumab (Bimzelx) showing durable symptom control and significant pain relief through three years in patients with moderate to severe HS.
Research published in PubMed highlights the role of IL-17A and IL-17F in the pathogenesis of hidradenitis suppurativa and the efficacy of bimekizumab as a dual inhibitor.
UCB is advancing galvokimig, a multi-specific antibody targeting IL-33, into Phase 2a clinical trials for COPD and non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis (NCFB).
Kymera Therapeutics initiated a Phase 2 trial of KT-474 (SAR444656), an oral IRAK4 degrader, in patients with moderate to severe HS in collaboration with Sanofi.
Kymera presented data on KT-579, a potent and selective oral IRF5 degrader, targeting systemic sclerosis (SSc) and other rheumatic diseases, highlighting its potential in fibrotic conditions.
Kymera's IRAK4 degrader (KT-474) and potentially its IRF5 degrader are noted in rheumatology pipelines for indications including Polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR).
A study published in the Journal of Investigative Dermatology demonstrates that IRAK4 is overexpressed in HS skin and correlates with inflammatory biomarkers, supporting the mechanism of KT-474.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics announced plans to initiate dosing of patients with hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) in H1 2027 using its lead VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader, MRT-6160.
Preclinical data for MRT-6160 was presented at ACR Convergence 2025, highlighting its potential to modulate immune pathways relevant to systemic sclerosis (SSc).
Monte Rosa Therapeutics entered a global licensing agreement with Novartis to develop MRT-6160, with potential applications in COPD and other immune-mediated diseases.
Preclinical studies of MRT-6160 showed significant reduction in soluble BAFF levels and inhibition of plasmablast differentiation, suggesting potential in B-cell driven autoimmune diseases.
Preclinical analysis of VAV1 degradation by MRT-6160 showed modulation of T-cell 'stemness' markers, including a significant impact on IL7R gene expression.
InflaRx reported positive Phase 2a data for INF904, an oral C5aR inhibitor, in hidradenitis suppurativa (HS). The company is also developing vilobelimab for HS.
A study published in PubMed highlights the clinical efficacy of complement C5a inhibition by IFX-1 (vilobelimab) in patients with moderate-to-severe hidradenitis suppurativa.
InflaRx is involved in research related to complement inhibition and glucocorticoid tapering in giant cell arteritis (GCA).
Cullinan Therapeutics announced a strategic expansion into autoimmune diseases, including plans to develop CLN-978, a CD19-directed T-cell engager, for systemic sclerosis.
Cullinan Therapeutics expanded its pipeline with the licensing of xeligekimab, a monoclonal antibody targeting IL-17, for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis and other autoimmune indications.
Nkarta is conducting a Phase 1/2 clinical trial (Ntrust-2) of NKX019, a CD19-directed CAR-NK cell therapy, in patients with systemic sclerosis.
Nkarta holds patents for BAFF-R and CD19 targeted chimeric antigen receptor-modified cells, exploring BAFF-R as a target for B-cell depletion.
Preclinical data for MRT-6160 at ECTRIMS 2025 discussed its impact on Th17 polarization and IL17 production in models of autoimmune disease.
Monte Rosa's preclinical data for VAV1 degradation by MRT-6160 was associated with modulation of IL33 signaling pathways.
Cullinan Therapeutics has indicated hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) as a potential target indication for its B-cell depleting therapies like CLN-978.
Cullinan Therapeutics was mentioned in a systematic review and update on PET imaging for polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR) and giant cell arteritis (GCA).

Candid Therapeutics announced a reverse merger with Rallybio Corporation. The combined company will focus on developing Candid's pipeline of T-cell engagers (CND261 and cizutamig/CND432) for autoimmune diseases, including systemic sclerosis (SSc).
Candid Therapeutics has partnered with WuXi Biologics to advance an innovative tri-specific antibody therapy targeting the TSLP/IL-7Rα pathway for autoimmune diseases.

Candid Therapeutics reportedly plans to initiate multiple Phase 2 studies following promising clinical data, with a focus on autoimmune and inflammatory conditions including Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD).

A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase 2 study of abiprubart (KPL-404), an anti-CD40 monoclonal antibody, met its primary endpoint in patients with moderate-to-severe Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS).

Kiniksa announced positive Phase 2 data for mavrilimumab (anti-GM-CSFRα) in Giant cell arteritis (GCA), demonstrating a significant reduction in the risk of flare and higher sustained remission rates compared to placebo.

Kiniksa's lead indication for mavrilimumab is Giant cell arteritis (GCA), but the company also explores its potential in related inflammatory conditions such as Polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR) and systemic sclerosis.

Vera Therapeutics reported positive long-term data from the ORIGIN Phase 3 trial of atacicept, a dual inhibitor of BAFF and APRIL, in patients with IgA Nephropathy. The FDA has granted Priority Review for its BLA.
Vera Therapeutics is identified as a key developer in the B-cell inhibitors market for non-oncology diseases, including Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS), through its work with atacicept.

Xencor determined the crystal structure of BAFF, a key target in autoimmune diseases, and developed a class of inhibitors using its 'Dominant Negative' protein engineering strategy to disrupt BAFF signaling.
Xencor holds patents for human anti-IL-33 neutralizing monoclonal antibodies, which incorporate XmAb engineered Fc variants for enhanced therapeutic activity in inflammatory conditions.
Xencor participates in the advisory and development efforts for IL-17 inhibitors, such as sonelokimab, an IL-17A/F nanobody, utilizing its XmAb technology to optimize antibody-like molecules.

Vor Bio entered into an exclusive global license agreement with RemeGen for telitacicept, a dual BAFF/APRIL inhibitor, for global development in multiple autoimmune indications including generalized myasthenia gravis (gMG) and Sjögren's Disease.
Vor Bio reported late-stage autoimmune asset telitacicept (BAFF/APRIL inhibitor) as a key program in its 2025 financial results and corporate update.
Scientific review discusses Ziritaxestat (GLPG1690) and its evaluation in systemic sclerosis and idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) trials.
A clinical study (NCT03976648) was conducted to test the long-term safety of GLPG1690 (Ziritaxestat) in participants with systemic sclerosis.
Galapagos' pipeline development mentions GLPG0778 as a JAK1 inhibitor explored for inflammatory diseases including COPD.
Alumis reported that oral ESK-001 (allosteric TYK2 inhibitor) demonstrated significant dose-dependent improvement in signs and symptoms of Hidradenitis Suppurativa.
Alumis' SEC 10-K filing mentions its intention to explore additional indications for its TYK2 inhibitor platform, including COPD and Systemic Sclerosis.
Alumis lists systemic sclerosis as a potential future indication for its precision immunology pipeline in its annual regulatory filings.
Q32 Bio is advancing bempikibart (ADX-914), a fully human anti-IL-7Rα antibody, for the treatment of autoimmune diseases such as Alopecia Areata and Atopic Dermatitis.
Q32 Bio identifies COPD as a potential future indication for bempikibart (anti-IL-7Rα) in its corporate strategy and SEC filings.
Incyte announced positive topline results from two Phase 3 clinical trials (STOP-HS1 and STOP-HS2) of povorcitinib in patients with Hidradenitis Suppurativa.
Incyte presented 54-week data for povorcitinib in Hidradenitis Suppurativa at the 2026 American Academy of Dermatology (AAD) Annual Meeting.
Incyte's baricitinib (Olumiant) has been evaluated in Giant Cell Arteritis (GCA) and other inflammatory conditions through its partnership with Eli Lilly.
Incyte's partnership with Eli Lilly includes development of baricitinib for indications such as Polymyalgia Rheumatica and GCA.
Research involving multi-omics profiling of chronic immune-mediated skin diseases mentions IL-17 and its relevance in conditions like Hidradenitis Suppurativa.
Multi-omics profiling of skin diseases like HS explores the role of cytokines such as IL-33 and IL-17 in disease pathogenesis.
Clinical trial (NCT07502872) protocol for Incyte/Genentech studies mentions COPD as a co-morbid condition requiring specific monitoring in treatment regimens.
